summary: Comprehensive testing of SIGMA iteration 2 features (repairs, new asset fields, exports, imports). Backend: 50/50 pytest cases pass (27 regression from iteration 1 + 23 new). Frontend: full Playwright coverage of dashboard import/export dropdown (CSV + XLSX downloads verified via download events), Import dialog with real CSV upload showing imported/skipped/errors report, Add Asset form with all new fields (jenis, golongan, status, tanggal_pembelian, nilai_pembelian), dashboard status badges (Lunas + Perbaikan for AST-001), asset detail Informasi Asset card showing new fields with Rp formatting, in-repair-badge in header appearing/disappearing with repair status, Repair section: create/edit/delete flow, table shows tanggal/kerusakan/tindakan/biaya (Rp)/teknisi/status, Export dropdown (CSV+PDF) works, History export dropdown (CSV+PDF) works on AST-001. All flows green.

minor.issue: parse_upload exceptions are swallowed and remapped to a generic 422 message. Consider logging the underlying error (server-side) for debugging malformed uploads. Not user-facing critical.

Import creates new asset docs directly via asset_repo.insert bypassing the AssetIn schema, so validation rules (min_length, kode_aset dup within the same file, status Literal) are enforced inline in import_rows. Currently comprehensive, but duplicate kode_aset APPEARING TWICE within the same file will only skip the second occurrence based on the DB check made before inserting the first — because both find_by_kode calls run before either insert lands in a transaction; still, in practice sequential awaits mean the first one is committed before the second checks, so this is fine. Worth a code comment.

issues: When both 'Ditempatkan' (green) and 'Dalam Perbaikan' (red) badges are shown side-by-side, on narrow viewports they wrap under the kode chip — cosmetic only, still readable.

action_items: Optional: log parse_upload exceptions in routes.py import_assets before returning 422 (currently swallowed).
Optional: add a code comment or in-file dup check in AssetService.import_rows describing that duplicate kode_aset within the SAME upload file are handled by sequential DB writes.
AST-100 'Meja Rapat Kayu Jati' from main-agent smoke test remains in DB — not cleaned up by me (belongs to main agent). Consider deleting if not needed.
critical_code_review_comments: routes.py: correctly places /assets/export and /assets/import BEFORE /assets/{asset_id} — verified via TestExports::test_assets_export_route_not_swallowed. Good.

services.py AssetService._enrich now computes in_repair via count query with status filter — O(1) per asset but adds an extra query per asset list. For 10 items/page currently fine. If page limit grows or dataset scales, consider a single aggregation.
schemas.py: AssetIn.status uses Optional[Literal[...]] — nice; validates negative nilai_pembelian via ge=0. Repair.biaya same treatment.
exports.py: PDF uses reportlab landscape A4, CSV uses utf-8-sig BOM (Excel-friendly). Good.
exports.parse_upload: silently truncates rows when headers count < row cells. Consider warning; low priority.
AssetService.import_rows: title-cases status ('lunas' → 'Lunas') before validating against literal — user-friendly. Good.
RepairSection.jsx: pagination is 5/page (matches spec). Export button correctly disabled when data.total is 0.
AssetFormDialog.jsx: on edit, does NOT show office/room selectors (correct — location moved via Mutasi Asset). New fields persist through both create and update endpoints.
ImportDialog.jsx: dropzone is a <button>, file input is hidden with accept='.csv,.xlsx'. Clean UX. Reset happens on close.
downloadFile in api.js: creates blob URL, extracts filename from Content-Disposition, revokes URL. Standard pattern. Works with cookie auth (withCredentials preserved).
Existing minor from iteration 1 still stands: CORS_ORIGINS='*' with allow_credentials=True is technically invalid combo per CORS spec but works because same-origin ingress. Not blocking.
seed_data_creation: Created UI-TEST-{ts} asset with all new fields, one repair, tested edit/delete/status flip — cleaned up. Import UI test uploaded a 3-row CSV (imported=1 UI-IMP-*, skipped=1 AST-001 dup, errors=1 invalid status) — imported asset cleaned up. AST-100 from main-agent smoke test left as-is.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 50 backend pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/{backend_test.py,test_new_features.py} are the regression baseline. Login selectors are login-email-input / login-password-input / login-submit-button (NOT login-email/password/submit). All new data-testids listed in the review request are present and functional. Export/import routes correctly registered BEFORE /assets/{asset_id}. downloadFile uses blob + Content-Disposition; verify in Playwright with page.expect_download. Repair pagination fixed at 5/page. History export button is disabled when history.total===0.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues identified. All feature and regression tests green.
